claimText: The Payments Regulatory Board is constituted under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
evidenceExcerpt: The RBI constituted the Payments Regulatory Board under the Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007.
examUse: Statement-based question on the statutory backing of the Payments Regulatory Board.

claimText: The RBI Governor serves as the ex officio Chairperson of the Payments Regulatory Board.
evidenceExcerpt: The Board consists of the RBI Governor (ex officio Chairperson), Deputy Governor, one RBI officer, and three central government nominees.
examUse: Statement-based question on the composition and leadership of the Payments Regulatory Board.

claimText: The Payments Regulatory Board includes three central government nominees who serve a 4-year tenure.
evidenceExcerpt: The Board includes three central government nominated experts with a 4-year tenure.
examUse: Statement-based question on the tenure and appointment of members in the Payments Regulatory Board.

claimText: The Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 designates the Reserve Bank of India as the central authority overseeing payment infrastructure in India.
evidenceExcerpt: The Payment and Settlement Systems Act, 2007 designates RBI as the central authority overseeing payment infrastructure in India.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the primary regulatory authority for payment systems in India.
distractorHooks: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)
